atoms.semantic_claim: The old Optimism signed transaction primitive exposes its raw signature through the SignedTransaction implementation, including for the OP transaction envelope type.

destinations.evidence: No exact OP signed transaction primitive exists in the port.
The port resolves `reth-primitives-traits` from crates.io 0.3.1; that trait has no `signature()` accessor and no OP transaction envelope implementation.
The active Ethereum/custom envelope has a raw `signature()` helper for standard, scheduled, async, and passkey variants, but this is not an OP deposit transaction API.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Confirmed gap, scoped to OP API compatibility: the old OP signed transaction `signature()` accessor is not present because the port does not carry OP transaction primitives. Active Ethereum/custom transaction signature access exists in local Alloy, so this is not evidence of a Ritual FSM/runtime gap.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: If OP transaction support becomes required, add a compile/API regression that can retrieve a raw signature from OP signed transaction envelopes, including deposit handling.
atoms.notes: Review closed as confirmed OP API/support gap only; not an active FSM issue in the current Ritual port.

summary: The old OP signed transaction `signature()` API is not preserved in the current port. Active Ethereum/custom signature helpers live in local Alloy, but OP transaction primitives are absent, so this is a confirmed non-FSM API gap.
notes: Review closed against current port code: no `crates/optimism/primitives/src/transaction/signed.rs` exists.
`reth-primitives-traits` is pulled from crates.io 0.3.1 and its `SignedTransaction` trait has no `signature()` method.
Local Alloy provides `EthereumTxEnvelope::signature()` for active custom Ethereum variants, so the gap is OP API compatibility, not active Ritual FSM behavior.
